records.ety: [L. conducere to bring together, conduce, hire; con- + ducere to lead. See Duke and cf. Conduct, n., Cond.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Conduced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Conducing.]

defs: To lead or tend, esp. with reference to a favorable or desirable result; to contribute; -- usually followed by to or toward.
quotes: He was sensible how much such a union would conduce to the happiness of both.
The reasons you allege do more conduce To the hot passion of distemper'd blood.

defs: To conduct; to lead; to guide.
quotes: He was sent to conduce hither the princess.
